title: Level 119
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Prata; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 119: "Under Renovation"}} {{Background|file=Level 147.png}} {{Transparent Theme}}
119
Under Renovation
{{Notice2|bg=#FFFFFF|tc=#343434|body=This page is designed to be read in Dark Mode.}}
{{Class Custom Image|class=Variable|hexagon=|color=#383838|safety=⋄ Physical Undone Space|security=⋄ Structurally Unstable|entity=⋄ Constantly Generating|bg-image=2017dc79d54414301e7e21b86a44a826.png}} {{Audio|round=yes|file=Building Construction Sound Effect SFX.mp3|align=center|loop=yes|volume=0.3}}
Appearance
{{Blurimage|file=Level 147.png|caption=A Newly Renovated Part of '''Level 119'''|align=left}} 'Under Renovation.,' Also known as '''Level 119''', is an endlessly expanding complex of house hallways and unfinished rooms in various stages of construction. The level constantly generates new sections. While some areas appear nearly finished, others remain little more than exposed frames and construction materials.''' The level is primarily constructed from drywall, stone, concrete, wooden beams, and unfinished flooring. Many rooms have stacks of building supplies, ladders, buckets of paint, and tools. {{Blurimage|file=6ea358c7df6d2f807b58bd890aabed8e.png|caption=A picture of a basement in '''Level 119'''|align=left}} Level 119 also has a large basements area located beneath the main floor. These basements are typically accessed through staircases or large holes in the floor. Unlike the upper sections of the level, the basement is primarily constructed from concrete, cinder blocks, wooden supports, and exposed pipes. The layout is also much more open, often consisting of large rooms connected by wide corridors. Hallways are also much more open and straight. The basement often appears older and less maintained than the rest of the level. Construction materials such as wood, drywall, paint buckets, and tools can frequently be found scattered across the floor. Many rooms appear abandoned midway through construction, with unfinished walls and exposed framing being almost found in every single room. The basement is significantly darker than the main floor. Windows are extremely rare, and most areas rely on ceiling lights for light. However, many of these lights are damaged, flickering, or doesn't work, leaving large sections of the basement in total darkness. {{Blurimage|file=2017dc79d54414301e7e21b86a44a826.png|caption=Water Damage in '''Level 119'''|align=left}} Large holes can occasionally be found in both the floors and ceilings. Some holes lead into unfinished basements or lower construction areas. Others appear to descend into a dark void with no visible bottom. The holes often appear to be the result of severe water damage, despite no water being present anywhere within the level. Objects dropped into these voids rarely produce any sound, regardless of how long one waits. The windows found throughout the level are completely white and cannot be seen through. Breaking a window reveals only more white space beyond the glass. The electricity within level 119 work throughout most of the level. Lights are usually functional, though flickering bulbs and sudden power outages can happen randomly. Some rooms may remain illuminated despite having no visible lamp or power source, while others rooms have light switches that appear to serve no purpose.
Renovation Periods
{{Blurimage|file=8972b4340d25750a371fdcc1917f170e.png|caption=Under Construction|align=right}} '''Renovation Periods''' are unpredictable events that can occur anywhere within Level 119. Shortly before one begins, the sounds of construction can often be heard throughout the area. Hammering, drilling, sawing, and heavy machinery may echo through walls, ceilings, and unfinished rooms despite no one ever being seen. As the event progresses, sections of the level begin to change. New walls may appear, doorways can become blocked, and entire rooms may be remodeled within a matter of hours. Inspection Tags are found in almost every room within '''Level 119'''. These tags are typically attached to walls, doors, or support beams. They classify how safe and stable an area is and come in three categories: Green, Yellow, and Red. Green Tags are found in most rooms and indicate that the area is considered safe. Yellow tags are found in rooms with minor issues or hazards, ranging from holes in the floor to unstable floors. Yellow tagged areas can be somewhat dangerous, with environmental hazards being almost guaranteed. Red tags are rare and are found in rooms with severe hazards, such as exposed wiring or extremely unstable structures. They usually indicate that a Renovation Period is right around the corner. The more notices that appear in an area, the more unstable it tends to become. Floors may crack, walls can collapse, and large holes may form without warning. In some cases, entire sections have been demolished and replaced with completely new layouts after the event ends. Electrical systems may also be affected during a Renovation Period. Lights can begin flickering, power may temporarily fail, and previously dark rooms have been known to suddenly become illuminated. Some doors may become locked for some time under the event. A Renovation Period can last anywhere from a few minutes to several hours. Once complete, the affected area remains permanently altered.
Entities & Hazards
{{Blurimage|file=707993c079e22a3c242e4a2de6b25158.jpg|caption=Early Stages Of Construction|align=left}} Level 119 is completely devoid of life, to the point of near complete silence. No entities have ever been confirmed within the level. It is widely believed that a person is the only living thing present while exploring the level. Despite this, sounds of construction can frequently be heard throughout the level. Talking can sometimes also be heard but is likely hallucination. These sounds often seem to come from behind walls, or from nearby rooms. The level continues to expand, renovate itself, and generate new sections despite appearing completely empty. The reason for this remains unknown. The only real hazard is the fact that the level is unfinished and still under construction. Missing floors, exposed wiring, unstable staircases, and large holes are common throughout the level.
Colonies & Outposts
Level 119 is affected by [[Phenomenon 15]], making it impossible for people to encounter one another. Each person will experience it completely alone and also completely different.
Addendum

DISCOVERY LOG.1
The following log details is Michael Foster and James Wilson trying to communicated via a radio within Level 119. ---- '''Date:''' 12/5/2003
'''Location(s):''' Level 119
'''People:''' Michael Foster and James Wilson
'''Uploaded by:''' Unknown ---- '''''' Michael Foster: James? Can you hear me? James Wilson: Loud and clear. Michael Foster: Good. I just entered a room with exposed drywall and a stack of paint buckets. James Wilson: That's odd. I'm in a room that looks exactly the same. Michael Foster: Do you see me? James Wilson: No. Michael Foster: Neither do I. [Around 20 minutes pass.] James Wilson: I found a staircase. Going up. Michael Foster: I found one too. James Wilson: Are we in the same place? Michael Foster: We should be. James Wilson: Then where are you? [A couple of minutes pass.] Michael Foster: I can hear hammering nearby. James Wilson: Same here. Michael Foster: It's coming from behind a wall. James Wilson: Mine too. Michael Foster: I'm going to check. [One minute passes.] Michael Foster: Nothing. Just an empty room. James Wilson: Same result. [Around 2 hours pass.] James Wilson: I've been following your directions exactly. Michael Foster: So have I. James Wilson: We should have crossed paths by now. Michael Foster: We should have crossed paths hours ago. [Silence for 30 seconds.] James Wilson: I don't think we're ever going to find each other. Michael Foster: Yeah. I think you're right. '''''' ---- The test continued for another four hours. Neither individual was able to locate the other. The experiment confirmed that direct contact between people within Level 119 is impossible.
Entrances & Exits
{{Blurimage|file=5b1d798b4351f1299a5d2b1513af0a97.png|caption=An Exit that leads to [[Level 28]]|align=right}} ===Clocking In=== *Remaining alone for several days on any level has a small chance of causing a noclip into Level 119. *Nocliping through a concrete wall on [[Level 960]] will lead here. *Opening a door marked "PRIVATE PROPERTY" in [[Level 11]] will lead here. *Other entrances is through a random noclip. ===Clocking Out=== *Finding a old poster and touching will lead to [[Level 123]]. *Finding a hole in the ceiling and crawling through it may lead to [[Level 28]]. *Certain doors may lead to [[Level 672]].
